title: Chicken Paprika
cScaleSummary: C2 because the only meaningful cholesterol-contributing ingredient is 12 ounces of boneless skinless chicken breast spread across 4 servings. The regular egg-free noodles, vegetables, silken tofu, olive oil, flour, stock, herbs, and spices contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL Active because the recipe uses lean chicken in a controlled portion, regular egg-free noodles, vegetables, olive oil, and silken tofu for body and creaminess, while building flavor through paprika blooming, browning, aromatics, and a glossy sauce rather than butter, cream, sour cream, or animal fat.
intro: Chicken paprika sits in the broad Central European family of paprika-rich chicken dishes, where the flavor comes from slowly softened onions, sweet paprika, and a warm red sauce that clings to noodles. This version keeps the comfort-food shape of the dish while using lean chicken, regular egg-free noodles, and a silken tofu finish to give the sauce body without turning it heavy.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Noodles
items: Bring {{quantity:water-noodles}} water and {{quantity:kosher-salt-noodles}} kosher salt to a boil in a medium pot.
Add {{quantity:regular-wide-noodles}} regular egg-free wide noodles and cook until tender according to the package timing.
Reserve 1/2 cup of the noodle cooking water, then drain the noodles and set them aside.

stepGroups.title: Season and Brown the Chicken
items: Season {{quantity:chicken-breast}} boneless skinless chicken breast with {{quantity:kosher-salt-chicken}} kosher salt, {{quantity:sweet-paprika-chicken}} sweet Hungarian paprika, and {{quantity:black-pepper-chicken}} black pepper.
Heat {{quantity:olive-oil-chicken}} olive oil in a wide skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken in a single layer and brown for 2 to 3 minutes per side, until the surface has color but the center is not fully cooked through.
Transfer the browned chicken to a plate. It will finish cooking in the sauce.

stepGroups.title: Blend the Creamy Base
items: Blend {{quantity:silken-tofu}} silken tofu, {{quantity:vegetable-stock}} low-sodium vegetable stock, and {{quantity:lemon-juice}} lemon juice until completely smooth.

stepGroups.title: Build the Paprika Sauce
items: Reduce the skillet heat to medium and add {{quantity:olive-oil-sauce}} olive oil.
Add {{quantity:yellow-onion}} yellow onion and {{quantity:red-bell-pepper}} red bell pepper. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until softened and lightly browned at the edges.
Add {{quantity:garlic-cloves}} garlic and cook for 30 seconds.

Pull the skillet briefly off the heat. Stir in {{quantity:sweet-paprika-sauce}} sweet Hungarian paprika, {{quantity:smoked-paprika}} smoked paprika, and {{quantity:all-purpose-flour}} all-purpose flour until the vegetables are evenly coated. Keeping the pan off direct heat during this step prevents the paprika from turning bitter.
Return the skillet to medium heat and slowly stir in the blended tofu-stock mixture.
Add {{quantity:kosher-salt-sauce}} kosher salt and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring often, until the sauce turns glossy and lightly thickened.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Chicken
items: Return the browned chicken and any plate juices to the skillet.
Simmer for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce clings to the pieces.
Add the drained noodles and toss gently to coat. Use the reserved noodle cooking water, 1 tablespoon at a time, only as needed to loosen the sauce so it coats the noodles without becoming soupy.
Finish with {{quantity:parsley}} fresh parsley and serve hot.
